jobs.name: Memory Curation
jobs.prompt: Memory curation for Sean. Review recent memory files, distill significant events/decisions into MEMORY.md. Identify anything worth promoting from daily logs to long-term memory. If nothing needs attention, reply HEARTBEAT_OK.

jobs.name: Morning Briefing
jobs.prompt: Morning briefing for Sean (Sydney, weekday). Check: Sydney weather, calendar events for today, any urgent emails or messages. Deliver a concise briefing. If nothing urgent, reply HEARTBEAT_OK.

jobs.name: Cross-Agent Bridge Poll
jobs.prompt: Cross-agent status check. Review recent agent activity across sessions, check for any stalled tasks, delegations that need follow-up, or issues requiring attention. Report briefly to Sean. If all clear, reply HEARTBEAT_OK.

jobs.name: System Monitor & Cleaner
jobs.prompt: System health check + cleanup for Sean. Check disk space, running processes, any failed services. Clean up temp files, old logs. Report status concisely. If all clear, reply HEARTBEAT_OK.

jobs.name: HKUDS/ClawTeam Daily Summary
jobs.prompt: HKUDS/ClawTeam daily summary. Fetch open PRs, recently merged PRs (last 7 days), open issues, and recent CI runs for HKUDS/ClawTeam repo. Post a formatted report to Discord channel #clawteam (channel ID: 1486920272547151922). Format with emojis: :red_circle: for open PRs, :green_circle: for merged, :clipboard: for issues, :gear: for CI. Include repo stats (stars, forks). Use the Discord bot token from environment.

jobs.name: Delegation Monitor
jobs.prompt: Delegation health check. Review any spawned subagent tasks, check for stalled or failed delegations, suggest config improvements. Report to Sean concisely.

jobs.name: Autonomy Digest
jobs.prompt: Autonomy digest. Review recent agent sessions and activity logs, summarize key accomplishments, any failures or errors encountered, and suggestions for workflow improvements. Report to Sean.

jobs.name: hermes-sync rolling rebuild
jobs.prompt:
Run the hermes-sync rolling update script. This script pulls the latest hermes-sync git repo, builds a new Docker image, pushes it to GHCR, and does a rolling restart of the hermes containers.

IMPORTANT: This script requires Docker CLI and GHCR authentication. It must be run from the host machine (not inside a container) with access to the docker daemon.

The script is at: ~/hermes-sync/scripts/sync-and-push.sh

Steps:
1. Run: cd ~/hermes-sync && bash scripts/sync-and-push.sh
2. If the script exits non-zero, check what failed
3. On success, the output will confirm the image was pushed and containers restarted

If docker is not accessible (running inside a container), output "SKIPPED: docker not available from this context" and exit 0.

jobs.name: Roadmap Autonomy Engine — Overnight (1am-7am Sydney)
jobs.prompt:
Run the Roadmap Autonomy Engine for Sean — the next-generation overnight engine.

Execute:
```
cd /opt/data/hermes-sync && git pull && python scripts/roadmap_engine.py --phase all
```

Deliver the final Phase 3 narrative report back here when complete.

jobs.name: Hermes Full Backup — Git sync
jobs.prompt:
Run the Hermes complete backup script (git sync only, no docker images).

Execute:
```
/usr/bin/python3 /opt/data/scripts/hermes-backup.sh >> /opt/data/logs/hermes-backup.log 2>&1
```

The script is at /opt/data/scripts/hermes-backup.py and backs up all hermes state (config, skills, memories, sessions, state.db, .env, etc.) to the ChonSong/hermes-sync GitHub repo. No ignores, complete backup.

Just run it — no reasoning needed. Log the result to /opt/data/logs/hermes-backup.log.

jobs.name: Hermes Full Backup — Docker Image
jobs.prompt:
Run the Hermes complete backup script INCLUDING Docker image backup.

Execute:
```
/usr/bin/python3 /opt/data/scripts/hermes-backup.sh --full-image >> /opt/data/logs/hermes-backup.log 2>&1
```

This backs up all hermes state AND saves Docker images (hermes-sync:latest, agent-os, postgres) to /opt/data/backups/docker-images.tar.gz. This takes several minutes and produces a ~5-6GB file that is then included in the next git sync.

Just run it — no reasoning needed.

jobs.name: seans-reporepo refresh
jobs.prompt:
Run the seans-reporepo catalog refresh on the host.

SSH into host: `ssh -i /home/hermeswebui/.hermes/container_key -o StrictHostKeyChecking=no sean@172.19.0.1`

Execute:
```bash
cd /home/sean/.hermes/cache/seans-reporepo
git pull origin main
python3 scripts/generate-catalog.py /home/sean/.hermes/cache/seans-reporepo
git add -A
if ! git diff --cached --quiet; then
    git commit -m "Auto-refresh: $(date '+%Y-%m-%d %H:%M UTC')"
    git push
    echo "Catalog updated and pushed."
else
    echo "No changes."
fi
```

The script generates: README.md, COMBINATORIAL.md, owned/*.md, starred/*.md, candidates/ (preserved).
Verify the changelog output shows star count changes and any new/removed repos.
Report the results: repos count, new stars, top star gainers.

jobs.name: context-budget-audit
jobs.prompt: Run a context budget audit on the Hermes skills system. Use Python to scan /opt/data/skills/ directory, count lines/words/tokens per skill, identify heavy skills (>400 lines), check for content overlap between skills with similar names, and produce a report. Save the report to /opt/data/reports/context-budget-YYYY-MM-DD.md and summarize findings including total tokens, top 10 heaviest skills, and 3 actionable recommendations for reducing token overhead.

jobs.name: agent-os-canary-watch
jobs.prompt: Run a canary watch check on agent-os at http://localhost:3001. Use browser_navigate to load the page, browser_console to check for JS errors, and curl to test the health endpoint. Report: HTTP status, response time, console error count, page title, whether navigation is present, and any visual issues. If anything is broken, flag it as CRITICAL. Save findings to /opt/data/reports/canary-watch-YYYY-MM-DD.md.

jobs.name: hermes-web-computer: rebuild + deploy check
jobs.prompt:
Rebuild and verify hermes-web-computer:

1. cd /opt/data/hermes-web-computer && git pull
2. cd frontend && npm run build
3. cd backend && go build ./... && go test ./...
4. If both pass: restart the server (kill old, start new on port 3005)
5. curl localhost:3005 to verify

Report: build time, test results, server status. If anything fails, report the error.

Working dir: /opt/data/hermes-web-computer

jobs.name: hermes-web-computer: Phase Engine (60m)
jobs.prompt:
Execute the next pending phase of hermes-web-computer using the Persistent Phase Engine.

STATE_DIR: /home/hermeswebui/.hermes/project-state/hermes-web-computer/
WORKDIR: /home/hermeswebui/.hermes/hermes-web-computer/
HERMES_HWC_ROOT: /home/hermeswebui/.hermes/hermes-web-computer
GOPATH: /home/hermeswebui/.hermes/home/go

Steps:
1. Read PHASE_TRACKER.json from STATE_DIR
2. Find the first phase where status != "complete" 
3. Verify git state of WORKDIR — if current phase was already committed by a prior run, skip to next
4. Execute the phase per its spec (build, test, verify)
5. On success: commit with phase name, write checkpoint to CHECKPOINTS/, update tracker, push
6. On failure: log error to checkpoint, stop — do NOT continue to next phase
7. Deliver summary to Discord: commit hash + what was done

Go binary: /home/hermeswebui/.hermes/home/go/pkg/mod/golang.org/toolchain@v0.0.1-go1.26.0.linux-amd64/bin/go
Always set GOPATH=/home/hermeswebui/.hermes/home/go when building/testing.
